title: Improve save+reload UX — avoid unnecessary disk re-reads

answers.prompt: What approach should we take for in-memory vs disk reads?
answers.text: In-memory filter — load once, navigate instantly

answers.prompt: After auto-tag/audit, how should we refresh?
Should we add a manual refresh button/keyboard shortcut?
answers.text: Yes — add a refresh button + keyboard shortcut

answers.prompt: When clicking an album in the sidebar, what should happen?
answers.text: Instant — no loading indicator for navigation

answers.prompt: After saving a single track's metadata, should we do anything extra?
answers.text: No reload — optimistic update is sufficient

answers.prompt: What is the scope of this change?
answers.text: In-memory navigation + scoped auto-tag reload + refresh button + rollback guard

notes: User raised concern about optimistic update rollback on failure — it could stomp concurrent edits or switch-track state. Include a freshness guard: only roll back if in-memory state still matches what was optimistically written.
